title: Solver fallback path: defer allocation, extract helper, validate objective
analysis:
Three related improvements to the warm-start fallback path in solver.rs, all touching the DimensionalSolver::solve() method (lines 476-679).

1. DEFER ALLOCATION (perf): `initial_fallback_values: Option<HashMap<_, _>>` is eagerly constructed at lines 518-537 on every warm-start solve (whenever initially_feasible=true). It allocates+populates a HashMap by zipping auto_params with initial values. However, this HashMap is only consumed in the error path at line 628 (`if let Some(fallback) = initial_fallback_values`). Since `initial: Vec<f64>` (line 488) stays in scope through the entire method, the HashMap can be built lazily inside the fallback block. This makes the success path zero-cost.

2. EXTRACT HELPER (DRY): The zip-params-with-values-into-HashMap pattern appears 3 times: (a) fallback construction lines 519-533, (b) early-exit lines 502-511, (c) solution construction lines 660-669. Additionally, `build_trial_values` (lines 55-67) does the same mapping but inserts into ValueMap instead of HashMap. Extracting `fn params_to_value_map(params: &[AutoParam], x: &[f64]) -> HashMap<ValueCellId, Value>` eliminates this duplication. `build_trial_values` can iterate over the helper's output to insert into the cloned ValueMap.

3. VALIDATE OBJECTIVE ON FALLBACK (correctness): The fallback return at line 635 (`SolveResult::Solved { values: fallback }`) bypasses the post-solve objective validation at lines 649-657. If the initial point has an undefined objective (div-by-zero, NaN, missing value), the fallback silently promotes it to Solved. The fix adds the same `eval_objective` check before the fallback return. The `trial_values` ValueMap (built from the initial point at line 489-490) is already in scope and can be reused for the objective evaluation.

steps.description: Write unit test `params_to_value_map_builds_correct_hashmap`: call the new helper with 2 AutoParams (one LENGTH, one ANGLE) and corresponding f64 values, assert the resulting HashMap has correct entries with correct ValueCellIds, si_values, and dimensions.

Extract `fn params_to_value_map(params: &[AutoParam], x: &[f64]) -> HashMap<ValueCellId, Value>` as a module-level helper. Refactor `build_trial_values` to call it and insert results into the cloned ValueMap. Refactor the early-exit path (lines 502-511) and solution construction (lines 660-669) to call `params_to_value_map` instead of inline loops. Do NOT change the fallback path yet (that comes with deferral). Run existing tests to confirm no regressions.

Write integration test `undefined_objective_at_feasible_initial_returns_no_progress`: create a problem where the initial point is feasible (x=10mm satisfies x>5mm) but the objective is always undefined (minimize(x/0)). Assert the solver returns `SolveResult::NoProgress`, never `Solved`. This test validates the contract that undefined objectives are never silently promoted to Solved, covering both the normal path (lines 649-657) and the fallback path (after fix).

Add objective validation to the fallback path: before `return SolveResult::Solved { values: fallback }` at line 635, add `if let Some(obj) = &problem.objective && eval_objective(obj, &trial_values, &problem.functions).is_none() { return SolveResult::NoProgress { reason: ... }; }`. The `trial_values` ValueMap (built from the initial point at line 489-490) is already in scope and represents the same values as the fallback.

Defer fallback HashMap allocation: remove the eager `initial_fallback_values: Option<HashMap<_, _>>` construction at lines 518-537. In the fallback block (line 628), replace `if let Some(fallback) = initial_fallback_values` with `if initially_feasible`, then construct the HashMap on-the-fly via `let fallback = params_to_value_map(&problem.auto_params, &initial);`. The `initially_feasible` bool and `initial: Vec<f64>` are both still in scope. This makes the success path zero-allocation. Run full test suite to confirm no regressions.

design_decisions.decision: params_to_value_map returns HashMap<ValueCellId, Value>, not ValueMap
design_decisions.rationale: SolveResult::Solved expects HashMap<ValueCellId, Value>. The 3 call sites that need deduplication (early-exit, fallback, solution construction) all produce HashMap. build_trial_values can iterate over the HashMap to insert into ValueMap — the overhead is negligible since auto_params is typically 1-10 entries.

design_decisions.decision: Reuse existing trial_values for fallback objective validation instead of rebuilding
design_decisions.rationale: trial_values (line 489-490) is built from the initial point and problem.current_values — the exact same data that the fallback represents. It is already in scope at the fallback block, so reusing it avoids an unnecessary build_trial_values call. The ValueMap uses persistent data structures, so it's cheap to keep in scope.

design_decisions.decision: Replace Option<HashMap> with bool guard for deferred construction
design_decisions.rationale: After deferring, initial_fallback_values is never needed as an Option. The bool initially_feasible (already computed at line 491-493) serves the same guard purpose. The HashMap is constructed lazily inside the fallback block only when needed (optimizer drifted infeasible). This eliminates the allocation on the success path entirely.

design_decisions.decision: Test undefined-objective-on-fallback via contract assertion rather than path-specific trigger
design_decisions.rationale: Triggering the fallback path (optimizer drifts infeasible) deterministically in a unit test is impractical — Nelder-Mead with penalty method strongly resists drifting infeasible. Instead, the test asserts the behavioral contract: 'feasible initial + undefined objective → NoProgress'. This holds on both the normal path (lines 649-657) and the fallback path (after fix). The fix is structurally obvious (same check copy-pasted) and doesn't need path-specific coverage.

reuse.how: Already used in build_trial_values and the inline zip patterns. params_to_value_map will reuse it for the same purpose.
Already used in cost function (line 422) and post-solve validation (line 652). The fallback objective check reuses it with the same pattern: eval_objective(obj, &values, &functions).is_none().

reuse.what: CompiledExpr::binop(BinOp::Div, x_ref, zero_int, Type::Real) pattern for undef objective
reuse.how: The new integration test reuses the same x/0 div-by-zero pattern to create an always-undefined objective expression.

reuse.what: Test scaffolding pattern (ValueCellId, AutoParam, CompiledExpr, ResolutionProblem)
reuse.how: New tests follow the same structure: create ids, build expressions, construct ResolutionProblem, call solver.solve(), match on SolveResult.
Already computed for early-exit and iteration budget decisions. Reused as the guard for deferred fallback construction, replacing the Option<HashMap> sentinel.
